name: New Zealand

visa_free.notes: Australian citizens and permanent residents do not need a visa or NZeTA. They are granted entry permission on arrival under the Trans-Tasman Travel Arrangement. No pre-travel authorization required.

visa_policy.notes: New Zealand's visa framework has three tiers: (1) Australian citizens and permanent residents are fully visa-free with no NZeTA required — they are granted entry permission on arrival under the Trans-Tasman Travel Arrangement; (2) Citizens/nationals of 61 visa waiver countries and territories must obtain a New Zealand Electronic Travel Authority (NZeTA) before travelling — this is classified under 'eta' as it is a mandatory pre-travel electronic authorization; (3) All other nationalities must apply for a visitor visa. The NZeTA is valid for 2 years for travellers (5 years for airline/ship crew). Standard stay on NZeTA is 3 months; UK passport holders may stay up to 6 months. Citizens of China and Pacific Islands Forum countries travelling from Australia with an eligible Australian visa may also use the NZeTA route. The official site lists 61 visa waiver jurisdictions but some have specific passport or residency conditions. Portugal and Taiwan appear in the official 61-country list but their waivers apply only to permanent residents — they are therefore recorded in conditional_access. Hong Kong and Macau waivers apply to residents on SAR passports, not all passport holders — this is noted in each entry.

conditional_access.conditions: Traveller must be travelling to New Zealand directly from Australia (must check in from an Australian airport; cannot be transiting through Australia). Eligible Australian visa must be valid at the time of check-in for the flight to New Zealand. NZeTA must be requested at least 72 hours before travel. Purpose of visit must not be for medical consultation or treatment. Scheme launched 3 November 2025; as of June 2026 it remains active and ongoing (over 80,000 Chinese and Pacific visitors had arrived under the scheme by May 2026). Cook Islands and Niue nationals are excluded as they already have visa-free access to New Zealand.

credential.subtype: passport holders with right to live permanently in Portugal

conditional_access.conditions: Visa waiver — NZeTA required. Applies to passport holders who have the right to live permanently in Portugal. Max stay 3 months.

credential.subtype: permanent residents travelling on Taiwan passports

conditional_access.conditions: Visa waiver — NZeTA required. Applies to permanent residents travelling on Taiwan passports only. Max stay 3 months.

cbi.notes: New Zealand has no Citizenship-by-Investment program. Investment visa pathways (Active Investor Plus Visa, Business Investor Visa) lead only to permanent residence, not direct citizenship. Citizenship requires 5 years of residence after obtaining a resident visa. Source: https://www.immigration.govt.nz/work/visas-for-investing-and-doing-business-in-new-zealand/ and https://www.immigration.govt.nz/visas/active-investor-plus-visa/

programs.name: Active Investor Plus Visa — Growth Category
programs.notes: Minimum NZD $5 million invested in higher-risk assets (managed funds, direct business investments, philanthropy max 20%) for 3 years. Applicant must spend minimum 21 days in New Zealand during the 36-month investment period. After meeting all conditions, can apply for permanent residence. New Zealand citizenship available after 5 years of residence. Application fee NZD $27,470. Processing time: 80% within 10.5 weeks for approval in principle. Accepts: direct investments, managed funds, philanthropy (max 20%). No changes to investment minimums confirmed as of page date March 2, 2026.

programs.name: Active Investor Plus Visa — Balanced Category
programs.notes: Minimum NZD $10 million invested in mixed assets for 5 years. Acceptable assets include direct investments, managed funds, listed equities, philanthropy, bonds, or property developments. Applicant must spend minimum 105 days in New Zealand during the 60-month investment period (reducible by 14 days per extra NZD $1 million invested, max 42-day reduction). Application fee NZD $27,470. Processing time: 80% within 10.5 weeks for approval in principle. No changes to investment minimums confirmed as of page date March 2, 2026.

programs.name: Business Investor Work Visa (Standard — NZD $1M)
programs.notes: Invest at least NZD $1 million in an acceptable New Zealand business actively operated by the applicant. Work visa valid up to 4 years. Requires maintaining employment levels and proving active management. Eligible for Business Investor Resident Visa after 3 years of operating the business. Even after residence approval, business must be actively run for at least 3 years in total. Application fee applies.

programs.name: Business Investor Work Visa (Fast-track — NZD $2M)
programs.notes: Invest at least NZD $2 million in an acceptable New Zealand business. Fast-track pathway to Business Investor Resident Visa eligible after 12 months of operating the business (not 3 years). However, business must still be actively run for at least 3 years in total even under the fast-track pathway. Permanent resident status available after holding resident visa for 2+ years and meeting all conditions. Launched 24 November 2025.

programs.name: Green List — Straight to Residence Visa (Tier 1)
programs.eligibility: Must have a job offer or current employment in a Tier 1 Green List role with an accredited employer. Must be aged 55 or younger. Must earn at least NZD $35.00/hour (the 2025 median wage) or the specified rate for the Tier 1 role. Position must be permanent or fixed-term for at least 12 months (or consecutive 6-month contracts). Must speak and understand English, be in good health, and have good character.
programs.notes: Provides immediate resident visa for Tier 1 Green List occupations (globally in-demand, nationally significant, in ongoing shortage). Permanent Resident Visa available after 2 consecutive years. New Zealand citizenship available after 5 years of residence. Application fee from NZD $6,450. The Green List is regularly updated; ten trades occupations were added to Work to Residence pathway on 18 August 2025. Wage threshold confirmed at NZD $35.00/hour (2025 median wage) as of March 2026.

programs.name: Green List — Work to Residence Visa (Tier 2)
programs.eligibility: Must have a job offer or current employment in a Tier 2 Green List occupation with an accredited employer. Must meet qualification, registration, and wage requirements for the specific role. Position must be full-time (at least 30 hours per week), genuine, and permanent or at least 12-month fixed term.
programs.notes: Work visa that provides a pathway to residence. Eligible for residence application after meeting work requirements. Part of the Green List pathway for in-demand occupations.

programs.name: Skilled Migrant Category Resident Visa
programs.eligibility: Must have an acceptable skilled job with an accredited employer (at least 30 hours/week, permanent or 12+ month fixed term, or consecutive 6-month contracts). Must meet one of: occupational registration requiring 2+ years training, bachelor's degree or higher, or income at 1.5x the median wage. Must accumulate 6 skilled resident points based on qualification level and New Zealand work experience. PhD/doctorate holders or those earning 3x median wage qualify immediately. Master's degree or 5-year registration: 5 points + 1 year NZ experience. Honours degree or 2x median wage: 4 points + 2 years NZ experience. Bachelor's degree, postgraduate certificate, or 1.5x median wage: 3 points + 3 years NZ experience.
programs.notes: Points-based system. No substantive eligibility changes noted on the official page as updated March 2, 2026. The prior record noted a future change effective 24 August 2026 reducing maximum required NZ work experience from 3 years to 2 years for bachelor's degree holders — this change was not confirmed on the official page fetched in June 2026 and is therefore moved to gaps.

programs.name: Talent (Arts, Culture, Sports) Work Visa
programs.eligibility: Must be aged 55 or younger. Must demonstrate exceptional talent in arts, sport, or culture with international reputation. Must secure an acceptable sponsor providing accommodation and financial support, and backing from a New Zealand organization of national repute in the field. Must be in good health and of good character.
programs.notes: Work visa valid up to 30 months. After 2 years of active engagement in the declared field, applicant may apply for the Talent (Arts, Culture, Sports) Resident Visa. Application fee from NZD $1,630. Partners and dependent children cannot be included in the work visa but may be added in the subsequent residence application.

agreements: Trans-Tasman Travel Arrangement (with Australia — freedom of movement for citizens and permanent residents)

validation_notes: CORRECTED: China/Pacific Islands scheme conditions updated — the '12-month trial' language removed as the scheme is active and ongoing as of June 2026 (80,000+ arrivals logged through May 2026 per immigration.govt.nz news centre). Note about Cook Islands and Niue exclusion added.
CORRECTED: Australia visa_free source_url updated to point to the main 'what-you-need-to-visit' page which explicitly confirms Australians need no NZeTA, rather than the visa-waiver-countries page.
CORRECTED: UK eta notes updated to use exact official wording ('UK citizens and other British passport holders who have the right to reside permanently in the UK') rather than paraphrase.
CORRECTED: Hong Kong and Macau eta notes updated to use 'residents travelling on' phrasing consistent with official wording (not 'HKSAR passports or British National Overseas passports only').
CORRECTED: Portugal conditional_access credential.subtype updated to use official wording ('passport holders with right to live permanently in Portugal').
CORRECTED: Active Investor Plus Visa notes updated to note that no changes to investment minimums were found on the page dated March 2, 2026.

CORRECTED: Business Investor Work Visa NZD $1M notes simplified — removed unverified job count detail (5 full-time jobs / 1 new position) and unverified age/residency detail not confirmed by the official page fetch; added confirmed detail that 3-year total business operation is required even under the fast-track pathway.

CORRECTED: Skilled Migrant Category Resident Visa eligibility rewritten to reflect the actual points table confirmed by the official page (March 2026 version) — the previously stated simplified description was incomplete.
CORRECTED: Skilled Migrant Category note about August 2026 change moved to gaps as it was not confirmed by the March 2026 official page fetch.
VERIFIED: 61-country visa waiver list count confirmed. Portugal and Taiwan correctly in conditional_access (permanent residents only). All other countries confirmed present on official list.
gaps: The official NZeTA page does not provide a comprehensive enumerated list of all eligible visa waiver countries in machine-readable form — the full 61-country list was compiled from the visa waiver countries page.
Specific processing times for the Skilled Migrant Category Resident Visa and the Green List Work to Residence Visa were not stated on the official pages fetched.

The official Business Investor Work Visa page (NZD $1M standard pathway) does not confirm the previously noted specific requirements of 5 full-time jobs maintained, 1 new position created, and under-55 age requirement. These details require verification against the full official eligibility page.

Dual citizenship rules for investor visa holders were not explicitly stated on the Active Investor Plus Visa official page — the general NZ dual citizenship policy (allowed unless the other country prohibits it) applies per govt.nz.
Visa-on-arrival is not an official New Zealand entry category — confirmed absent.
Diplomatic/service/official passport-only bilateral visa waiver agreements were not confirmed from official sources with sufficient specificity and are therefore absent from conditional_access.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder exemption found on any official New Zealand immigration page — this category does not appear to apply to New Zealand.

The Skilled Migrant Category change effective 24 August 2026 (reducing required NZ work experience from max 3 years to max 2 years for bachelor's degree holders) was not confirmed on the official page fetched in June 2026 — may have been rolled back, deferred, or not yet reflected on the public page.
NZeTA fee schedule: the page indicated 'From NZD $17' but a full breakdown of current fees (including the International Visitor Conservation and Tourism Levy component) was not extracted.

outbound_official.note: no official New Zealand government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. The NZ Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade (mfat.govt.nz/en/embassies/passports-and-visas) explicitly states it cannot provide outbound visa/entry requirement information for NZ citizens and directs travelers to consult destination-country embassies. SafeTravel (safetravel.govt.nz) and Passports (passports.govt.nz/travel-information) similarly contain no consolidated outbound visa-free list — only per-country travel advice pages without structured visa-level classifications. No origin-side official list analogous to India's MEA vffin page exists for New Zealand.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa
visa_types.purpose: Tourism, visiting family, short business meetings
visa_types.notes: Apply online via Immigration New Zealand. Stay up to 9 months in an 18-month period. Fee approximately NZD 165.

visa_types.name: Student Visa
visa_types.purpose: Studying in New Zealand for longer than 3 months
visa_types.notes: Requires offer of place from a New Zealand educational institution. Fee approximately NZD 275.

visa_types.name: Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV)
visa_types.purpose: Working for an accredited New Zealand employer
visa_types.notes: Employer must be AEWV-accredited. Fee approximately NZD 700. Pathway to residence via Skilled Migrant Category.

visa_types.name: Working Holiday Visa
visa_types.purpose: Holidaying and working in New Zealand (age 18–30/35 for select nationalities)
visa_types.notes: Available for nationals of 45+ countries. Many nationalities can apply online from anywhere. Fee approximately NZD 250.
